---
title: "eDNA & Environmental Metabarcoding"
subtitle: "Topic hub"
---

Environmental DNA (eDNA) — genetic material shed into water, soil, or air — lets us detect and characterize communities without capturing organisms. This is a growing thread in the course, with its own lecture, assignment, and an optional project track.

## Module materials

- **Lecture:** [eDNA & Metabarcoding](lectures/edna.qmd)
- **Assignment:** [eDNA Metabarcoding pipeline](https://github.com/sr320/fish546-2026-assignments/tree/main/assignments/08-edna-metabarcoding)
- **Question set:** [Week 08](https://github.com/sr320/fish546-2026-assignments/blob/main/questions/week08.md)

## What you'll learn

- **Concepts:** what environmental nucleic acids are, how samples are collected and filtered, and why contamination control matters.
- **Markers:** common metabarcoding markers (COI, 12S, 16S, 18S) and when to use each.
- **From reads to biology:** amplicon QC → **DADA2** (amplicon sequence variants) vs. OTU clustering → taxonomic assignment against reference databases → an abundance / occurrence table.
- **Ecological readouts:** diversity, species detection and occupancy, and comparing communities across sites or time.
- **Caveats:** false positives/negatives, index hopping, and reproducibility concerns specific to eDNA.

## Where it runs

The heavy steps (denoising, taxonomic assignment) run on **Hyak/Klone** — this is exactly why you complete the [Hyak tutorial](tutorials/03-hyak.html) before class. Interactive exploration and figures happen on **Raven** or your own machine.

## eDNA project track

You may take an eDNA dataset as your quarter research project: raw amplicons → community table → figure/report, using the same GitHub, issue-tracking, and compendium workflow as every other project. Flag this in your **project-proposal** issue.

## Competency anchors (Roberts Lab Handbook)

- [Bioinformatics Basics](https://robertslab.github.io/resources/bio_Basics/)
- [Annotation](https://robertslab.github.io/resources/bio-Annotation/)
- [Genomic Resources](https://robertslab.github.io/resources/Genomic-Resources/)
- [Data Management](https://robertslab.github.io/resources/Data-Management/)
